units.title: Form 1: Fundamentals of Computing
learningOutcomes: Demonstrate understanding of essential computer software and hardware
Demonstrate proper management of operating system and files
Diagnose and resolve common computer problems
suggestedActivities: Discussing history and evolution of computers
Classifying types of computers and types of software
Analyzing components of computer hardware
Discussing functions of an operating system and demonstrating proper handling
Installing and updating the operating system
Applying file management tasks such as copying, pasting, renaming and deleting
Organizing files and folders using appropriate naming conventions and directory structures
Identifying and installing commonly used application software
Analyzing computer error messages and isolating common computer problems
Accessing help resources and recovering lost data from corrupted or damaged storage devices
assessmentMethods: Understanding of essential computer hardware and software components demonstrated correctly
Proper management of operating system and files demonstrated correctly
Commonly used application software appropriately operated

units.title: Form 1: Productivity Tools - Word Processing
learningOutcomes: Create documents using word processing software
suggestedActivities: Exploring the word processing interface and describing its basic functions
Demonstrating correct typing skills
Creating and formatting documents using text editing features such as font styles, paragraph alignment and spacing
Inserting and manipulating visual elements like tables, images and charts
Applying templates to design professional documents
Utilizing tools for reviewing and editing documents
Importing and exporting documents in different formats
assessmentMethods: Documents successfully created using word processing software

units.title: Form 1: Data Representation
suggestedActivities: Discussing the basic concepts of number systems including binary and decimal
Exploring the role of number systems in computer systems
Converting between number systems
Analyzing binary code to understand its representation in real world applications
Demonstrating the use of binary numbers in programming and hardware configurations
assessmentMethods: Number systems correctly applied in computing

units.title: Form 1: Computer Networks
suggestedActivities: Discussing basic concepts of computer networks
Identifying basic networking devices and software
Discussing types of connections
Configuring a local area network (LAN) using basic networking devices such as routers and switches
Implementing basic network sharing settings for devices such as printers, files and folders across the network
assessmentMethods: Computer networks correctly configured and installed

units.title: Form 1: Cybersecurity
learningOutcomes: Apply online safety and security measures
suggestedActivities: Discussing terminologies and key concepts in cyber security
Identifying different types of cyber threats such as hacking, phishing and social engineering attacks
Detecting potential cyber threats using monitoring tools and logs
Describing the importance of cyber security in the digital world
Implementing strong password policies and multifactor authentication to protect user accounts
assessmentMethods: Online safety and security measures successfully applied

units.title: Form 1: Data Processing
suggestedActivities: Discussing key concepts of data processing and cleaning
Exploring types of data in computing and identifying data processing techniques
Discussing tools for capturing and cleaning data
Collecting data from multiple sources and demonstrating how to clean and validate data
Designing visualizations to present processed data (Power BI, Tableau, Google Data Studio)
Developing testing protocols to ensure accuracy and consistency of processed data

units.title: Form 1: Web Design
learningOutcomes: Design and structure web pages using web design tools
suggestedActivities: Discussing the key concepts of web design
Identifying the components of a web page
Describing the roles of HTML, CSS and JavaScript in creating and designing web pages
Designing and structuring simple web pages using HTML and semantic tags
assessmentMethods: Simple web pages and structures successfully implemented

units.title: Form 1: Digital Citizenship
learningOutcomes: Demonstrate ethical behavior in a digital environment
suggestedActivities: Discussing digital citizenship
Demonstrating respectful communication in online interaction
Distinguishing between fact, opinion and bias in digital content
Demonstrating how to balance screen time with offline activities to maintain physical and mental wellbeing
Demonstrating a culture of inclusion, respect and fairness in online communities
assessmentMethods: Ethical behavior in a digital environment demonstrated appropriately

units.title: Form 1: Databases
suggestedActivities: Defining databases and discussing key concepts such as tables, records, fields and keys
Differentiating types of databases and describing database models
Identifying popular database software (Access, Oracle, MariaDB)
Creating tables and defining their structures using database tools

units.title: Form 1: Artificial Intelligence
suggestedActivities: Defining AI
Discussing the core concepts of AI including machine learning, deep learning and natural language processing
Identifying the problems to be solved and determining objectives of the model
Collecting and preparing datasets for creating AI models
Using simple machine learning to create AI models

units.title: Form 1: Internet of Things (IoT)
topics: Basic Components of Internet of Things (IoT)
suggestedActivities: Discussing the concepts of IoT
Identifying key components of IoT systems such as sensors, actuators, microcontrollers and communication modules
assessmentMethods: Understanding of IoT components demonstrated appropriately

units.title: Form 1: Logic Gates
suggestedActivities: Discussing the concept of logic gates and their role in digital circuits
Identifying types of logic gates and their symbols
Analyzing combinational and sequential logic gates
Working with truth tables
Creating a simple digital control system such as an alarm system or traffic lights

units.title: Form 2: Productivity Tools - Spreadsheets
learningOutcomes: Use spreadsheets for data entry and analysis
suggestedActivities: Discussing the basic components of the spreadsheet interface (rows, cells, shortcuts)
Performing arithmetic operations using basic formulas and functions
Filtering and sorting data to identify trends, patterns or specific values
Performing statistical analysis using functions (COUNTIF, AVERAGE, MEDIAN)
Creating charts to visualize data
Protecting cells, sheets and workbooks
Sharing spreadsheets using cloud-based collaborative tools and creating macros
assessmentMethods: Data entered and analyzed correctly using a spreadsheet

units.title: Form 2: Data Representation
suggestedActivities: Discussing concepts of hexadecimal and octal number systems
Converting numbers between systems
Converting text using ASCII
Developing financial models using what-if scenarios

units.title: Form 2: Introduction to Programming
suggestedActivities: Exploring different types of block-based programming software (Scratch)
Describing key features of block-based programming such as drag-and-drop blocks and visual workspace
Identifying basic programming constructs including loops, conditions, variables and events
Constructing simple programs by sequencing blocks to create specific outcomes

units.title: Form 2: Networking
suggestedActivities: Discussing different types of network topologies
Analyzing the roles of network topology
Creating network cables (Ethernet UTP, STP, FTP)
Designing, creating and configuring network topologies
assessmentMethods: Network topologies developed and configured correctly

units.title: Form 2: Cybersecurity
suggestedActivities: Discussing cyber security and its importance in digital systems
Investigating cyber vulnerabilities in operating systems, software and hardware
Using cybersecurity tools and technologies
Developing policies to protect data, devices and networks from cyber threats
assessmentMethods: Cybersecurity tools and technologies utilized accordingly

units.title: Form 2: Algorithms
suggestedActivities: Identifying different types of algorithms
Discussing characteristics of algorithms
Designing flowcharts and pseudocode
Using different types of algorithm design techniques

units.title: Form 2: Web Development
suggestedActivities: Defining Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) and explaining their purpose in web design
Identifying the different types of CSS (inline, internal and external)
Describing the basic structure of a CSS rule including selectors, properties and values
Using text properties to style fonts including font family, font size and color
Applying background properties such as background color, background image and background repeat
Styling borders and spacing using properties like border, margin and padding
assessmentMethods: CSS concepts applied to websites correctly

units.title: Form 2: Database
learningOutcomes: Design and create databases using SQL
suggestedActivities: Discussing Structured Query Language (SQL) concepts
Writing SQL scripts to create and modify database structures
assessmentMethods: Databases designed and created using SQL correctly

units.title: Form 2: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
suggestedActivities: Exploring the problems to be solved
Selecting suitable models
Designing and developing the model
Evaluating the models

units.title: Form 2: Internet of Things
suggestedActivities: Discussing different development platforms and exploring the user interfaces
Creating code for IoT devices
Connecting IoT devices using wireless communication protocols like WiFi, Bluetooth, Zigbee or LoRaWAN
Building a basic IoT system using sensors, microcontrollers (Arduino, Raspberry Pi) and actuators (LEDs)

units.title: Form 2: Robotics
suggestedActivities: Discussing the basic concepts of robotics including ethical use of robots
Classifying types of robots
Identifying the core components of a robot mechanism
Designing a basic robotic system considering functionality, form and purpose

units.title: Form 3: Productivity Tools - Presentation and Desktop Publishing
learningOutcomes: Create and format desktop publishing publications
suggestedActivities: Exploring and identifying different types of presentation software
Creating presentations using templates and themes and applying design principles
Embedding hyperlinks and other advanced interactive elements
Discussing and identifying different types of publications and publishing software (Publisher, Adobe, Canva)
Designing, laying out, creating and formatting publications

units.title: Form 3: Logic Gates
suggestedActivities: Simulating logic gate circuits using digital design software (CircuitVerse)
Analyzing the output of logic circuits for various inputs
Implementing Boolean expressions using logic gates
Integrating logic gates into microcontroller-based systems for automation
Designing sequential circuits for time-dependent applications (counters, timers)

units.title: Form 3: Networking
suggestedActivities: Exploring the internet and its key components (search engines, servers, clients)
Discussing internet services
Setting up accounts on different platforms for professional and personal use
Engaging in video conferencing and webinars using platforms such as Zoom and Google Meet
Creating and sharing online content using different internet platforms
assessmentMethods: Online content created and shared appropriately

units.title: Form 3: Cybersecurity
learningOutcomes: Mitigate and respond to cyber threats
suggestedActivities: Discussing data protection laws (GDPR, CCPA) in handling user information
Analysing the impact of online actions on personal and professional reputation
Applying encryption techniques to secure sensitive data during storage and transmission (symmetric and asymmetric encryption)
Employing Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) to secure remote connections
Developing security policies and guidelines to ensure compliance with best practices
assessmentMethods: Cyber threats mitigated and responded to correctly

units.title: Form 3: Programming
suggestedActivities: Identifying various Integrated Development Environments (IDEs) such as Jupyter and PyCharm
Exploring Python syntax and control structures
Installing and configuring Python IDEs (VS Code, Jupyter)
Writing Python scripts using conditional statements (if, else, else-if) to manage decision making
Creating Python projects for task automation, data analysis and web development
assessmentMethods: Python used to code programs correctly

units.title: Form 3: Web Development
suggestedActivities: Discussing concepts of JavaScript and its role in web development
Analyzing JavaScript and other web technologies
Integrating Object Oriented Programming (OOP) principles in JavaScript
Creating animations using JavaScript and CSS transitions
assessmentMethods: Dynamic interactive web pages developed accordingly

units.title: Form 3: Database
suggestedActivities: Discussing key components of database management
Configuring user roles and permissions to control access to database resources
Upgrading and migrating databases to newer versions or platforms
Applying the first, second and third normal forms to organize data
Integrating a database with programming languages (Python, Java, PHP)

units.title: Form 3: Artificial Intelligence
suggestedActivities: Discussing deployment tools
Training and testing models
Deploying models
Monitoring and maintaining models

units.title: Form 3: Internet of Things (IoT)
suggestedActivities: Developing IoT dashboards to visualize sensor data in real time
Exploring real world IoT applications (transportation, education, healthcare, agriculture, smart homes)
Developing IoT solutions for a chosen sector

units.title: Form 3: Mobile Application
suggestedActivities: Exploring mobile applications and explaining their use in daily life
Discussing native, web and hybrid mobile applications
Identifying core features of mobile applications
Discussing common tools and programming languages used in app development
Analyzing common security risks in mobile applications and describing strategies for protecting personal data

units.title: Form 3: Cloud Computing
learningOutcomes: Develop and manage cloud computing models
suggestedActivities: Defining the key concepts and models of cloud computing
Discussing the benefits and challenges of cloud computing for business and individuals
Designing and developing cloud computing models
Managing the three main cloud services using modeling tools such as AWS EC2, Google Cloud and Microsoft Azure
assessmentMethods: Cloud computing models correctly developed and managed

units.title: Form 3: Robotics
suggestedActivities: Designing robotic system structures using CAD software
Installing and aligning robotic motors and gears
Setting up power supply systems for robots
Testing electronic circuits to ensure proper functioning of robotic components
Writing programs to control movement using languages (Python, C)
Combining mechanical, electronic and software components into a functional robotic system
Integrating robotics with Internet of Things (IoT) systems for remote monitoring

units.title: Form 4: Computer Systems
suggestedActivities: Discussing the key components of computer systems
Exploring different types of computer systems and their functions (expert system, batch processing system, control system)
assessmentMethods: Understanding of computer systems demonstrated accordingly

units.title: Form 4: Databases
suggestedActivities: Discussing the purpose and functions of a database management system
Identifying and installing common DBMS software (MS Access)
Creating databases
Inserting, updating and deleting data in tables

units.title: Form 4: Computer Networking
suggestedActivities: Discussing key networking concepts including OSI layers and TCP/IP models
Explaining the role of DNS, IP addressing and MAC addressing in networking
Discussing key networking protocols (TCP, UDP, HTTP, FTP, DHCP)
Implementing VLANs to segment network traffic for efficiency and security
Designing subnets to optimize IP address allocation using subnet masks
Configuring static and dynamic IP addresses on devices
Configuring Virtual Private Networks (VPNs) and Quality of Service (QoS)
Implementing encryption protocols such as WPA for wireless networks
assessmentMethods: Computer networks correctly designed and configured

units.title: Form 4: Programming
suggestedActivities: Discussing the main concepts of the C programming language
Describing the structure of a C program (headers, functions, main)
Writing simple programs using basic syntax and structures in C
Creating programs that use loops, arrays and strings for data storage and manipulation
Defining classes and objects in C to encapsulate data and behavior
Applying techniques to identify and fix errors
Writing C programs for object oriented applications like simulations and GUIs

units.title: Form 4: Mobile Application Development
suggestedActivities: Setting up and designing environments using tools (Android Studio, Xcode, VS Code)
Writing code to implement app functionality using programming languages (Java, Kotlin)
Designing and building responsive app interfaces that adapt to screen sizes and orientations
Integrating device features (GPS, camera and sensors) into mobile apps
Building offline capabilities using caching or local databases

units.title: Form 4: Emerging Technologies
suggestedActivities: Exploring emerging technologies and their role in transforming industries
Identifying key emerging technologies (AI, IoT, AR, VR)
Applying emerging technologies into industries
Discussing the potential impact of emerging technologies on society, economy and daily life
Discussing the concept of 3D printing and its role in manufacturing and design
assessmentMethods: Understanding of emerging technologies demonstrated appropriately

units.title: Form 4: Cloud Computing
suggestedActivities: Launching cloud-based applications and services including databases, storage and compute resources (DynamoDB, Firestore, Azure)
Designing cloud-native applications using microservices architecture
Using APIs for cloud-based services to integrate with other systems
Integrating cloud services with Internet of Things (IoT) devices for real-time data processing
Designing and implementing cloud migration strategies

metadata.source: Computer Science Syllabus, Secondary Education Ordinary Level, Form 1-4 (ISBN 978-9982-54-005-7)
metadata.authority: Curriculum Development Centre (CDC), Ministry of Education, Republic of Zambia
metadata.extractionNote: Transcribed from the official CDC PDF. The PDF's embedded font ships no ToUnicode map and no space glyphs, so text was recovered by decoding the font's fixed +29 character offset and re-segmenting words from the syllabus tables (TOPIC / SUBTOPIC / SPECIFIC COMPETENCES / LEARNING ACTIVITIES / EXPECTED STANDARDS). Content is transcribed from the source, not paraphrased or generated.
